title: Proxy Sync Engine
description: Inbound and outbound sync operations keep local proxy state consistent with the hub via timer-driven polling and flushing.

actors.name: ProxyNode
actors.description: The local proxy instance that owns the SyncEngine; initiates start/stop and notifies of new outbound messages

actors.name: LifecycleManager
actors.description: Provides AuthError and triggers re-authentication via onAuthError callback

actors.name: MailboxStore
actors.description: Local persistent store; provides cursors, pending queues, and message state

use_cases.name: Start Sync Engine
use_cases.description: Initialise timers for outbound flush (500 ms initial) and inbound poll (1 s initial); set _running=true

use_cases.name: Accelerate Outbound Flush
use_cases.description: When a new outbound message is queued, reschedule the outbound timer to fire in 100 ms instead of waiting the full interval

use_cases.name: Flush Outbound Messages
use_cases.description: Poll up to 50 pending outbound messages from MailboxStore and POST them to Hub /a2a/mailbox/outbound. Handle accepted, failed/rejected (with retry up to 10), and inline response messages. Update last_sync_at.
use_cases.next_interval_logic: 1 s if pending remain, else 5 s (DEFAULT_OUTBOUND_INTERVAL)

use_cases.name: Pull Inbound Messages
use_cases.description: POST to Hub /a2a/mailbox/inbound with cursor & limit=50; write received messages to MailboxStore; advance cursor.
use_cases.next_interval_logic: 10 s active, 60 s idle (>5 min since last activity)

use_cases.name: Handle Auth Error
use_cases.description: On HTTP 401/403 from Hub, throw AuthError; SyncEngine catches it and invokes onAuthError callback to trigger re-authentication

use_cases.name: Acknowledge Delivered Messages
use_cases.description: After each inbound pull cycle, list messages in 'delivered' status and POST their IDs to Hub /a2a/mailbox/ack

use_cases.name: Retry Failed Outbound Message
use_cases.description: If Hub rejects a message and retry_count < MAX_RETRIES (10), call store.incrementRetry(); otherwise mark as 'failed'

use_cases.name: Write Inline Hub Response as Inbound
use_cases.description: If a flush result contains a 'response' field, write it as an inbound message to MailboxStore so the proxy can consume it

use_cases.name: Detect Idle State
use_cases.description: Compare Date.now() - _lastActivity against IDLE_THRESHOLD (5 min); result controls inbound poll frequency

relationships: OutboundSync depends_on MailboxStore (pollOutbound, updateStatusBatch, writeInboundBatch, incrementRetry, setState)
InboundSync depends_on MailboxStore (getCursor, setCursor, getState, writeInboundBatch, list)
important_notes: SyncEngine uses unref'd timers (Node.js pattern — won't block process exit). _lastActivity tracks both sent and received events to manage idle detection. Inline Hub responses from the outbound flush are injected directly into the inbound store, enabling request-reply patterns. Protocol version is carried on every Hub request (PROXY_PROTOCOL_VERSION from mailbox/store).
